rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. POPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION $4130.95 U.S. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Petitioners First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; Petitioners First Set Of Special Interrogatories; Judicial Council Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 20, 2018, Line 1, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Compel Answers To Petitioners First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; Petitioners First Set Of Special Interrogatories; Judicial Council Form Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted. Claimant Jocobi Perkins shall provide code complaint responses to Petitioner's First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; Petitioners First Set Of Special Interrogatories; Judicial Council Form Interrogatories within 10 days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION: $59,143.00 U.S. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Final Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 20, 2018, Line 2, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's Motion For Final Judgment. Petitioner's motion for final judgment is GRANTED. Petitioner followed the proper notice procedures for a petition for forfeiture. (See Health & Safety Code §11488.4(a), (c), (e).) Interested parties Lian Cong Yu and Hui Qin Zhang failed to file any claim opposing forfeiture as required by Health & Safety Code §11488.5(a) (despite being served with the petition 20 months ago) or any opposition to the motion. Health and Safety Code §11488.5(b)(1) directs mandatory default when there is no timely claim opposing forfeiture. Even were interested parties' declarations taken as an opposition to the motion (which they are not), they fail to rebut the People's prima facie case for forfeiture. Probable cause is the showing for a prima facie case when respondent fails to file a claim opposing forfeiture. (See People v. $47,050 (1993) 17 Cal.App.4th 1319, 1325, n.6.) Probable cause is established by Sergeant Smith's declaration recounting the vehicle search and arrest of Yu on October 29, 2016, including the hidden large sum of cash, the packaging for and large amount of marijuana and the two canisters of cocaine. Yu fails to provide sufficient specific evidence to rebut the presumption based on probable cause of a nexus between the funds and narcotics transactions. (See id. at 1323-25.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for movant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ALLAN QUITON VS. BRIAN MCWILLIAMS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions, Set No. 2 And To Form Interrogatories, Set No. 4 (Interrogatory No. 17.1); Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 20, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANT BRIAN MCWILLIAMS, POTS 'N WATER, LLC's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions, Set No. 2 And To Form Interrogatories, Set No. 4 (Interrogatory No. 17.1); Request For Sanctions. I rule on defendant Brian McWilliams' motion to compel further responses to his second set of requests for admission as follows: RFA 4: DENIED. The request is vague and ambiguous as to time and its use of the word "skippered." In any event, an adequate response was provided. RFAs 5, 6, 11, 12: DENIED. The responses are adequate. RFA 13: GRANTED. RFAs 29, 30, 31, 33, 35, 36, 45, 46, 49, 50, 57, 59, 60: DENIED. The responses are adequate. I rule on McWilliams' motion to compel further responses to his fourth set of interrogatories (form interrogatory 17.1, as it regards certain requests for admission) as follows: RFA 4: GRANTED. RFA 5: DENIED. The RFA was adequately admitted. RFA 6: GRANTED. RFA 7: DENIED. The interrogatory response as to this RFA was not identified in notice of motion and RFA was adequately admitted. RFAs 11, 12: GRANTED. RFA 13: GRANTED (unless plaintiff's amended response to RFA 13 is an unqualified admission). RFA 14: DENIED. RFA was adequately denied on lack of personal knowledge. RFAs 29, 30, 31, 33, 35, 36: DENIED. Plaintiff's objections are valid. RFA 43: DENIED. The RFA was adequately admitted. RFAs 45, 46, 49, 50, 59: DENIED. Plaintiff has adequately responded to these RFAs, which largely seek contentions rather than facts. RFA 60: GRANTED. No sanctions are awarded to either party. Many of defendant's queries were of dubious litigation value and others have already been covered in plaintiff's deposition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for McWilliams is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RBU)

Counsel for Stanford-Startx Fund is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RBU)
